One Link
How it works Features Security Live mesh Share Builders About Get One Link
Get One Link

One tap. It just works.

We detected your device and prepared the right build. Signed, reproducibly built, and downloaded through the network itself instead of a normal HTTP fetch. The proof is right under the button.

Detecting your device...
Download for macOS ↓

The download opens a real One Link session with the release relay, streams the binary chunk by chunk, and verifies the signature in your browser as it arrives. If your network blocks anything alien, it falls back to a plain signed download silently.

Windows 59 MB .exe, today macOS notarizing Linux packaging Android packaging iOS via TestFlight OpenBSD build from source Source AGPL, today

honest status: no signed binary has been published to the release relay yet. the protocol works today; signed builds are in the queue. building from source works on every platform right now.

version0.21.0
released2026-05-12
sha256f905eef1b8c2a934d8e7c9a1e5d6b2f3c8a9d4e7b5c2a1f6e9d3b8a4c7e2f1d5
signatureEd25519 + ML-DSA-65 hybrid verified
attestationol_confidential + field witness fresh
buildreproducible, in sealed environment
verifyread attestation chain

No account. No email. No tracking. Free forever.

Why the proof matters

You should not have to trust us.

Every other download page says "we promise we are safe." We do not ask for your trust. We give you the math.

Signed twice.

Every binary carries a regular Ed25519 signature and a post-quantum ML-DSA-65 signature. Both must verify or the binary will not even open.

Built in a sealed room.

The build happens in a confidential-compute environment. The attestation document proves the exact source commit, the exact build environment, and that nothing else touched it.

Reproducible.

Anyone can build the same source and get the same byte-for-byte binary. If you and we both arrive at the same hash, no human has touched the result.

The download itself

It is already One Link.

The button you tap does not fetch a file over HTTP. It opens a real One Link session between your browser and the release relay, negotiates a hybrid key, then streams the binary chunk by chunk through the same protocol your phone will use tomorrow.

You can watch every step. Or ignore all of this and the file appears in your downloads folder. Both are fine.

// every download, every time
1. open session  browser → relay
2. hybrid key    X25519 + ML-KEM-768
3. request       one-link-<your-os>.bin
4. stream chunks AEAD per chunk, rekey every N
5. verify sha    matches advertised hash
6. verify sig    Ed25519 + ML-DSA-65 both ok
7. attest        ol_confidential fresh, witness ok

done  file written to disk
Private by default

You can also download anonymously.

Tap the toggle and the binary takes a three-hop route through volunteer relays on its way to you. Nobody, not even us, knows who pulled it. Same file, same hash, slower by a beat.

We do not log your visit. We do not log the request. We do not log the relay. If law enforcement asked us tomorrow who downloaded this, we would have nothing to give them. That is not a policy. It is the architecture.

computing 

      

    

  


  
  

    

      Or build it yourself
      
The whole thing is in the open.

      

        AGPL-3.0. Every line of the daemon, every protocol crate, every shader, every word of this site. Clone it. Read it. Fork it. Run your own relay.
      

      

        
          See the source on GitHub

          One Link
        
        
A free, private network for messages, files, and devices. Owned by no one. Belongs to everyone.
